mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth signals consumer demand strength. When retail sales are elevated, corporate earnings improve, driving S&P 500 forward returns higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS above median are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Extremely high unemployment rates signal severe labor market slack, depressing consumer spending and corporate profits. When UNRATE exceeds 8%, the S&P 500 forward return over the next quarter is negative.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when UNRATE > 8% are not significantly negative (t-test of mean return less than 0 at 5% level).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero short-term interest rates and rising inflation erodes real returns and signals a loss of monetary control, which is negative for equity market valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% and CPIAUCSL month-over-month change is above 0.3%, 20-day forward returns are not negative on average (mean return >= 0).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a leading indicator for economic recovery. In the early recovery phase after a recession, rising housing starts signal improving construction activity, job growth, and consumer confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns during periods of HOUST increasing from trough are not positive at a statistically significant level, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects underlying economic output. Higher industrial production indicates expanding economic activity and corporate profits, boosting equity prices over a short horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO month-on-month change versus 5-day forward returns is less than 0.05 over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25%, it signals an aggressive monetary easing stance that reduces discount rates and encourages risk-taking, resulting in positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not positive on average (mean <= 0).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high unemployment and declining GDP reinforce each other, creating a negative feedback loop that depresses corporate earnings and equity prices. This labor-demand interaction is particularly damaging.
failure_condition: When both UNRATE > 7% and two-quarters GDPC1 growth < 0%, the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not negative (mean >= 0).

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment below 3%, moderate increases in the CPI index signal healthy demand without overheating, which is supportive for equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: When CPI year-over-year changes are between 1% and 3%, the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not positively correlated with CPIAUCSL month-over-month changes (Spearman < 0.10).
